title: Zscaler Analytics API Reference | Zscaler Automation Hub
text:
Analytics API ReferenceZscaler Analytics
Zscaler Analytics API Reference

Zscaler Analytics GraphQL API for analytics and reporting.

Getting Started​

To learn more about setting up and using the Zscaler Analytics API, see Working with the Zscaler Analytics API.

Endpoint​
https://api.zsapi.net/zins/graphql

Authentication​

Include your API key in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer YOUR_BEARER_TOKEN

API Domains​

This API is organized into domains, each representing a specific area of functionality:

SaaS Security - SaaS Security contains queries for CASB data. Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) provides data and threat protection for data at rest in cloud services. (1 query)
Cyber Security - Cybersecurity is the state of being protected in cyberspace, including measures taken to protect computer systems against unauthorized access or attack. It refers to the policies, processes, and technologies to protect networks, devices, and data from cybercrime and data breaches. This domain provides data, including incident responses. (2 queries)
Zero Trust Firewall - Zscaler Zero Trust Firewall protects web and non-web traffic for all users, applications, and locations with the industry’s most comprehensive cloud-native security service edge (SSE) platform. This domain provides report data for specific type, like location, action. (5 queries)
IOT - Securing IoT begins with knowing which devices are connected to your network and what they’re doing. Zscaler IoT Device Visibility extends the power of the Zero Trust Exchange™ platform, using AI/ML to automatically detect, identify, and classify IoT devices across your estate. This domain provides device data and statistics. (1 query)
Shadow It - Shadow IT discovery (unsanctioned apps in use, which increases risk for an organization) report data. (2 queries)
Web Traffic - ZIA Web traffic report data or specific type, like protocols and threat categories. (5 queries)
Using Domains​

Access domain queries through the root Query type:

query {
  DOMAIN_NAME {
    # Domain-specific queries here
  }
}

Introspection​

You can explore the schema programmatically:

query IntrospectionQuery {
  __schema {
    queryType { name }
    mutationType { name }
    types {
      name
      kind
      description
    }
  }
}
